key_insight: Density-based topology optimization can design plasmonic metasurfaces that maximize attractive optical trapping forces on nanoparticles under normal light incidence.
engineering_relevance: The approach enables selective, potentially manufacturable nanoparticle trapping structures for biosensing, microfabrication, and quantum system assembly.
method: Use Maxwell stress tensor (MST) to evaluate optical forces on nanoparticles.
Optimize metasurface topology for maximum gradient force under monochromatic normal-incidence excitation.
Add manufacturing constraints to convert free-form layouts into planar designs.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimized designs for different nanoparticle sizes and materials, showing that trapping stiffness increases for smaller nanoparticles.
watch_item: Performance depends strongly on nanoparticle size and material, so a design optimized for one target may not generalize well to others.
paper_title: Topology optimized plasmonic metasurfaces for optical trapping of nanoparticles
